cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The options are A to D. First, I need to find the part of the text where it talks about her experience at the National Championships. Let me go through the text again. The passage says: "Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and she won the junior section. Full of confidence, she entered the National Championships, and although she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she came nowhere. 'I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running.'" So the key points here are that during the National Championships, she followed someone and made a mistake in the course, leading her to cycle extra kilometers. This mistake caused her to not finish well. The text mentions she ended up cycling 20 km too far. Looking at the options: A) She was tricked by another competitor. The text says she was following a man, but it doesn't say he tricked her intentionally. They both realized they went wrong, so it might have been a mutual mistake, not a trick. B) She felt she had let her teammates down. The text doesn't mention her teammates here; the story is about her individual mistake in the race. C) She made a mistake during part of the race. This seems correct because she went the wrong way, leading to extra cycling. D) She realized she couldn't cycle as fast as she
